# B03: Decisions Made

## Decision 1: V Will Intro Alex to Ben and Rob (Zo Founders)

**DECISION:** V will make a warm introduction between Alex Forman (Parse) and Ben and Rob (Zo founders) to explore a partnership/integration.
**CONTEXT:** Parse's web-to-API product is highly aligned with Zo's user base — non-technical power users who want to pull data from arbitrary websites into their Zo environment. V sees a natural fit between Parse's usage-based pricing model and Zo's consumption-oriented community.
**DECIDED BY:** Mutual agreement — V proposed, Alex consented ("Yeah, nice.")
**IMPLICATIONS:** Ben/Rob will receive an intro and likely schedule a follow-up call with Alex. This could lead to a Parse integration within the Zo ecosystem (e.g., bundled into V's N5OS starter repo or featured in the Zo Discord).
**ALTERNATIVES CONSIDERED:** None discussed — the fit was immediately apparent to both parties.

## Decision 2: Parse Switching to Usage-Based Pricing

**DECISION:** Parse will move from credit-based monthly buckets to usage-based pricing, likely within the week of the meeting.
**CONTEXT:** The current monthly bucket model doesn't fit well because costs per API call are highly variable — depending on site complexity, anti-bot measures, result volume, and bandwidth. Some users only need one-off extractions and shouldn't pay monthly.
**DECIDED BY:** Alex Forman (Parse founder) — this was already in motion internally.
**IMPLICATIONS:** Makes Parse more attractive for Zo integration since Zo's model also favors usage-based consumption. Removes friction for occasional users. Aligns pricing with actual cost structure.
**ALTERNATIVES CONSIDERED:** Monthly credit buckets (current model) — being abandoned due to poor fit with variable cost structure.

## Decision 3: V Will Demo Parse Use Cases and Provide Feedback

**DECISION:** V will build and share Parse demos on Zo within the next 1-2 weeks, along with product feedback.
**CONTEXT:** V wants to demonstrate real-world Parse usage within the Zo ecosystem — both to validate the integration hypothesis and to provide founder-to-founder product feedback. Parse also just released a major update with "a few kinks" being worked out.
**DECIDED BY:** V proposed, Alex welcomed it enthusiastically.
**IMPLICATIONS:** V becomes an early power-user tester for the new Parse release. Demo artifacts could become part of V's N5OS starter kit if the integration proves valuable. Feedback loop established between Parse and a key ICP representative.
**ALTERNATIVES CONSIDERED:** None — this was a natural next step.

## Decision (IMPLIED): Zo Discord as Distribution Channel for Parse

**DECISION (IMPLIED):** Both parties aligned that the Zo Discord (~1,000 members) is a relevant channel for Parse to engage with its ICP, with V endorsing Alex joining and participating authentically.
**CONTEXT:** V noted the Discord community is "very much in your ICP" — non-technical builders who would benefit from turning websites into APIs. V explicitly cautioned against spamming but encouraged genuine participation.
**DECIDED BY:** Consensus — V suggested, Alex expressed interest ("I want to try out the product myself").
**IMPLICATIONS:** Alex will likely join the Zo Discord as both a user and potential partner. This is organic community engagement, not a formal marketing arrangement.
**ALTERNATIVES CONSIDERED:** None explicitly discussed.
